fields.purpose: Globally unique immutable identifier used as primary key across Neo4j, Qdrant, and all cross-system references. Enables deterministic deduplication and cross-layer traceability.
fields.allowed_values: String matching ^CRULE-[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]{3,}$
relationships_to_other_fields: Referenced by sequence_prerequisite_ref in other rules
Used as the Neo4j node identity key

fields.purpose: Classifies rule semantics to select correct validation logic, evaluation model, and required field set. The compliance engine routes rule evaluation by rule_type.
fields.allowed_values: Enum: min_version_constraint, known_issue_fixed, readiness_requirement, feature_support_added, incompatible_combination, update_order_constraint
validation_rules: Must be one of 6 defined values
relationships_to_other_fields: Determines which fields are required (sequence_step required for update_order_constraint)
Restricts allowed severity values
Sets minimum confidence threshold

fields.purpose: Controls lifecycle state machine position, production eligibility, and allowed operations. The compliance engine only evaluates approved rules; Neo4j and Qdrant only import approved rules.
fields.allowed_values: Enum: candidate, validated, approved, deprecated, rejected, superseded, archived
validation_rules: Must be one of 7 defined states
relationships_to_other_fields: approved_by and approved_at are required when status=approved
Determines import eligibility for Neo4j and Qdrant

fields.purpose: Identifies the primary entity the rule applies to. Provides the RC2 entity ID enabling cross-layer graph linkage, plus version constraint for compliance evaluation.
fields.allowed_values: Object with entity_id (RC2 pattern), component_name, knowledge_category, version_constraint (operator, version_normalized, version_scheme, requirement_kind)
validation_rules: entity_id must exist in RC2 registry
operator must be from allowed set
version_normalized must be valid for declared version_scheme
knowledge_category must match RC2 entity category
relationships_to_other_fields: Must differ from object (no self-reference)
entity_id links to Layer 1 canonical entity via TARGETS relationship

fields.purpose: Defines the semantic relationship type between subject and object. The Neo4j generator uses this as the edge type. The compliance engine interprets the semantics to determine evaluation logic.
fields.allowed_values: Enum of 20 registered predicates: REQUIRES, SUPPORTS, CONFLICTS_WITH, FIXED_BY, UPGRADE_TO, DEPENDS_ON, REMEDIATES, BLOCKS, SUPERSEDES, REFERENCES, HAS_CONDITION, HAS_EXCEPTION, HAS_EVIDENCE, HAS_REMEDIATION, VALIDATED_BY, APPROVED_BY, DERIVED_FROM, REPLACES, TARGETS, SUPPORTED_BY
validation_rules: Must be from the 20-item controlled vocabulary
Direction is always subject to object
relationships_to_other_fields: Determines semantic meaning of the subject-object pair
Neo4j uses this as the edge label between entity nodes

fields.purpose: Identifies the secondary entity the rule constrains. Same structure as subject. The compliance engine compares the installed version of this entity against the version_constraint.
fields.allowed_values: Same object schema as subject field
validation_rules: entity_id must exist in RC2 registry
version_normalized must be valid for declared scheme
relationships_to_other_fields: Must differ from subject (self-reference forbidden)
entity_id links to Layer 1 entity via TARGETS relationship

fields.purpose: Defines the applicability gates. The compliance engine evaluates all conditions before firing the rule. Empty array means the rule applies universally.
fields.allowed_values: Array of condition objects with condition_id, entity_id, component_name, operator, version_normalized, version_scheme, condition_context
validation_rules: Each condition_id must be unique within the rule
operator must be from allowed operator set
version_normalized required when operator is version-comparative
relationships_to_other_fields: Combined using condition_logic (AND/OR)
incompatible_combination rule_type enforces minimum 2 conditions

fields.purpose: Declares scoped exclusions where the rule does not apply. Prevents false-positive compliance failures on exempt device families or configurations.
fields.allowed_values: Array of exception objects with exception_id, scope_type, excluded_entity_ids, excluded_device_families, justification

examples.justification: Device family lacks the affected NIC controller hardware.

validation_rules: scope_type must be from allowed set
relationships_to_other_fields: Connected via HAS_EXCEPTION relationship
Exceptions require evidence to be approved

fields.purpose: Captures runtime or install-time operational dependencies that context-qualify the rule. Used by the compliance engine for dependency chain analysis and by the Root Cause Analysis engine.
fields.allowed_values: Array of dependency objects with dependency_id, entity_id, component_name, operator, version_normalized, dependency_type
validation_rules: entity_id must exist in RC2 registry
dependency_type must be from allowed set
relationships_to_other_fields: Connected via DEPENDS_ON relationship in Neo4j
Used by Root Cause Analysis engine to trace dependency chains

fields.purpose: Explicitly records component-version combinations that produce documented negative outcomes when co-existing with the subject. Drives the compliance engine prohibited state detection.
fields.allowed_values: Array of conflict objects with conflict_id, entity_id, component_name, operator, version_normalized, conflict_symptom, severity
validation_rules: entity_id must exist in RC2 registry
relationships_to_other_fields: Connected via CONFLICTS_WITH relationship in Neo4j
Cross-reference with incompatible_combination rules for consistency

fields.purpose: Provides document-level traceability for every rule. Required before a rule can be approved. The source_excerpt is the primary text payload for Qdrant semantic embedding.
fields.allowed_values: Array of evidence objects with evidence_id, source_type, source_document_id, source_chunk_id, source_excerpt, confidence_score, extraction_method

examples.source_excerpt: Driver Pack versions prior to 12.4.0 were not validated.

validation_rules: At least 1 evidence required when status=approved
relationships_to_other_fields: Connected via HAS_EVIDENCE relationship
source_excerpt is the Qdrant embedding payload
source_document_id must match source_document field

fields.purpose: Prescribes actionable corrective actions. The Recommendation Engine uses remediation_hint for human-readable instructions; sequence_order enables ordered remediation plans.
fields.allowed_values: Array of remediation objects with remediation_id, remediation_type, target_entity_id, target_component_name, target_version, operator, sequence_order, remediation_hint

examples.remediation_hint: Upgrade Driver Pack to 12.4.0 or later.

validation_rules: target_entity_id must exist in RC2 registry
relationships_to_other_fields: Connected via HAS_REMEDIATION relationship
sequence_order used with sequence_step for ordered upgrade plans

fields.purpose: Document-level provenance anchor. Must match source_document_id in at least one evidence record. Used for document-level audit trails.
fields.allowed_values: String matching ^DOC-[A-Z0-9]+$
validation_rules: Should match at least one evidence[].source_document_id
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be consistent with evidence[].source_document_id entries
Connected via DERIVED_FROM or REFERENCES relationship to CompatibilityDocument node

fields.purpose: Sub-document reference for precise traceability within the source document. Enables pinpointing the exact chunk, page, or section that contains the supporting text.
fields.allowed_values: String (chunk ID, page reference, or section heading)
relationships_to_other_fields: Provides more precise location than source_document alone
Optional complement to evidence[].source_chunk_id

fields.purpose: Overall rule confidence synthesized from extraction quality and evidence reliability. Gates validation — rules below the rule_type minimum are rejected.
fields.allowed_values: Float between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive
relationships_to_other_fields: Minimum threshold determined by rule_type
Should be consistent with the highest evidence confidence_score

fields.purpose: Drives compliance alert priority and remediation urgency. The compliance engine maps severity to alert levels; the recommendation engine uses it to prioritize fix ordering.
fields.allowed_values: Enum: critical, warning, info
validation_rules: Must be one of critical, warning, info
relationships_to_other_fields: Restricts allowed values based on rule_type
Drives urgency_level in remediations (critical severity -> urgency: immediate)

fields.purpose: Boolean combinator for the conditions array. Tells the compliance engine whether ALL conditions (AND) or ANY condition (OR) must be true for the rule to fire.
fields.allowed_values: Enum: AND, OR
relationships_to_other_fields: Applied across all entries in the conditions array
Affects rule firing semantics in the compliance evaluation engine

fields.purpose: Position in a multi-step update sequence chain. Required for update_order_constraint rules. Enables the compliance engine to enforce ordered update execution.
fields.allowed_values: Positive integer >= 1
validation_rules: Must be absent for all other rule types
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be <= sequence_total_steps when both present
Pairs with sequence_prerequisite_ref for chain traversal

fields.purpose: Total chain length of the update sequence. Enables the compliance engine and recommendation engine to know when a sequence is complete.
fields.allowed_values: Integer >= 2
relationships_to_other_fields: Complements sequence_step for chain progress tracking

fields.purpose: Links this rule to its prerequisite step in the sequence. Enables the Root Cause Analysis engine to trace sequencing violations back to the blocking step.
fields.allowed_values: String matching rule_id pattern
validation_rules: Must not create circular reference chains
relationships_to_other_fields: Stored as BLOCKS edge in Neo4j
Used by Root Cause Analysis engine for chain traversal

fields.purpose: Links the rule to a specific CVE or internal security issue record. Enables security tracking, patch management integration, and compliance reporting against known vulnerabilities.
fields.allowed_values: String in format SEC-YYYY-NNN or CVE-YYYY-NNNNN
relationships_to_other_fields: Relevant primarily for known_issue_fixed rule_type
Used by compliance engine for CVE-level reporting

fields.purpose: Classification and quality tags for workflow management, filtering, and review routing. The unverified_value tag blocks approval without explicit human clearance.
fields.allowed_values: Array of strings from the defined tag vocabulary
validation_rules: Rules with unverified_value tag require human review before approval
relationships_to_other_fields: unverified_value tag blocks automatic approval promotion
Tags can be used as Qdrant filters for batch operations

fields.purpose: Audit trail anchor. Records when the rule was first ingested. Used for retention policy enforcement and rule aging analysis.
fields.allowed_values: ISO-8601 UTC datetime string
validation_rules: Must not be modified after creation
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be <= updated_timestamp
Must be <= approved_at when approved

fields.purpose: Records the most recent modification. Used to detect stale candidate rules and enforce the 90-day candidate expiry policy.
fields.allowed_values: ISO-8601 UTC datetime string
validation_rules: Must be updated on every field change
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be >= created_timestamp
Drives 90-day candidate expiry policy enforcement

fields.purpose: Governance record of the human reviewer who approved this rule. Provides the auditability proof required for production import.
fields.allowed_values: Non-empty string identifier of an authorized reviewer
validation_rules: Must be null when status is candidate or validated
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be present simultaneously with approved_at when status=approved
Stored via APPROVED_BY relationship in Neo4j

fields.purpose: Timestamps the formal governance approval decision. Required for production import eligibility. Used by the audit trail and retention policy engines.
fields.allowed_values: ISO-8601 UTC datetime string
validation_rules: Required and valid ISO-8601 when status=approved
Must be null when status is candidate or validated
relationships_to_other_fields: Must be present simultaneously with approved_by when status=approved
Must be >= created_timestamp
